step.title: Handle Redirects and Strike Escalation
step.goal: Classify the user's response and dispatch accordingly. The classification controls whether the skill confirms and proceeds, loops back to a prior phase, increments the strike counter, or recommends abort. Strike counter enforces Lock 31's three-strike escalation on foundational reversals.

substep.title: Classify the Response
substep.action:
Refine {{session.user_response.classification_hint}} into a final classification:
- **confirm**: user accepted the design; proceed to step 5 - **redirect_scope_ungrilled**: user revealed a scoping concern NOT previously raised in Q↔R. Loop back to ticket-grill with the new focus. - **adjust_chain**: user modified the artifact chain (added or removed an artifact, reordered). NOT a foundational reversal — edit the draft and re-present. - **adjust_risk**: user modified a risk-area statement. NOT a foundational reversal — edit and re-present. - **foundational_reversal**: user reversed a previously LOCKED foundational decision. Examples: - Changing a confirmed scope ("actually, the work should NOT include the auth changes after all") - Reversing a DR's decision ("I want to reconsider the shared-tables vs RLS choice") - Rejecting a confirmed Q↔R outcome on a load-bearing question STRIKE COUNTER INCREMENTS. - **abort**: user explicitly wants to stop. Archive the bundle and exit.
Use the verbatim record from substep 3b as the source of classification. When ambiguous, prefer the LESS severe classification (adjust over reverse; reverse over abort) but verify with one clarifying question.

check.action:
Append a note to {{session.questions_path}} (Q&A entry) describing the new scoping concern. Stamp last_updated on the questions file via stamp-frontmatter.py.
Present the user with: "This redirect surfaces a scoping gap not previously grilled. Recommend looping back to ticket-grill for an additional Q↔R turn focused on this gap. Confirm?"
On confirm: exit this skill cleanly. The user invokes ticket-grill via the ticketsmith menu (TG). ticket-grill's step 1 will re-load the bundle and re-enter the loop at step 2.
On decline (the user wants to proceed without grilling): treat as a force-skip; log in the questions file; loop back to step 2 of THIS skill with the redirect absorbed (assume the user knows what they're doing).
Edit the draft in {{session.design_summary_draft}} per the user's correction. Loop back to step 3 to re-present the adjusted version.

Increment {{session.foundational_strikes}}. Append a strike entry to {{session.strike_log}}: { strike_n: {{session.foundational_strikes}}, timestamp: ISO 8601, reversed_decision: , user_rationale: , agent_assessment: }
Apply the strike-N protocol: - Strike 1 → substep 4c - Strike 2 → substep 4d - Strike 3 → substep 4e

substep.title: Strike 1 Protocol
substep.action:
Strike 1: ABSORB and LOOP BACK.
Present to user: "Acknowledged — this is a reversal of a previously locked {{decision_type}}. I'll absorb the change. Identifying which Q↔R turns or research findings are invalidated: {{agent's list of invalidated bundle content}}
Loop back recommendation: ticket-grill, to re-grill the affected scope. Alternatively: stay in this skill and re-draft the design with the new constraint."
Append a strike-1 record to the questions file. Stamp last_updated.
Ask user to confirm direction: loop to ticket-grill OR stay in ticket-design with the absorbed reversal.
On loop: exit cleanly; user invokes ticket-grill via menu. On stay: loop back to step 2 of this skill with reversal absorbed.

substep.title: Strike 2 Protocol
substep.action:
Strike 2: SURFACE CONCERN.
Present to user: "Second foundational reversal this session. The bundle is becoming inconsistent — prior turns are being invalidated. Recommend pausing to consolidate before continuing.
Options: - Pause: review the bundle content; reconcile contradictions before next phase - Continue absorbing: proceed past this reversal (strike counter advances; one more reversal triggers abort recommendation) - Abort now: archive bundle as research evidence; start fresh Q↔R later"
Append a strike-2 record to the questions file. Stamp last_updated.
Ask user to confirm direction. On pause: exit cleanly with a pause note. On continue: loop back to step 2 with reversal absorbed. On abort: proceed to substep 4f.

substep.title: Strike 3 Protocol
substep.action:
Strike 3: RECOMMEND ABORT.
Present to user: "Three foundational reversals this session. This pattern indicates the original Q↔R scoping was wrong — accumulated inconsistencies in the bundle now exceed what additional absorption can repair.
Recommendation: ABORT this ticket-writing session. Archive the current bundle (status: abandoned, with a postscript explaining the abort) as research evidence. Start a fresh ticket-writing session with the new understanding from these reversals.
Options: - Abort and archive (Recommended): mark the ticket status=abandoned; add a postscript; exit - Override and continue: I'll force one more attempt; strike counter logic ends; you accept the inconsistency risk - Pause: stop here without aborting; resume later with fresh context"
Append a strike-3 record to the questions file. Stamp last_updated.
Ask user to confirm. On abort: proceed to substep 4f. On override: loop back to step 2 with reversal absorbed; do NOT increment strike counter further. On pause: exit cleanly.

substep.title: Abort Path
substep.action:
Edit the ticket frontmatter: set status = abandoned. Append a postscript section to the ticket body:
## Session Abandoned
- Reason: {{abort reason: explicit user abort OR three-strike escalation}} - Strikes accumulated: {{session.foundational_strikes}} - Strike log: see {{session.questions_path}} (entries timestamped this session)
This bundle is preserved as research evidence. To start a fresh ticket-writing session on the same underlying request, invoke ticket-orient with a new slug and reference this bundle as prior art in the Orient Summary.
Stamp last_updated on the ticket file with note: "Session abandoned per ticket-design step 4."
Exit the skill cleanly. Bundle is preserved; status reflects the abandonment.

section.title: The classification call is the core skill craft
section.description:
Most session-ending mistakes come from misclassifying a redirect as adjustment (under-counting strikes) or misclassifying an adjustment as reversal (over-counting strikes). When ambiguous, the agent asks ONE clarifying question before applying the strike protocol:
"Just to be precise: are you reversing the previous decision on X (which would be a foundational change), or adjusting the artifact-chain to include something new (which is additive)? These trigger different protocols."
The user's answer determines the classification.

rules.rule: Reverses a DR's chosen alternative → foundational
Reverses a Q↔R outcome on a load-bearing question (one whose answer drove other findings) → foundational
Changes the scope statement in a way that invalidates prior research turns → foundational
Adds a new artifact to the chain (ADR/UX spec) without contradicting prior decisions → adjustment
Restates a risk area more precisely → adjustment
Reorders the artifact chain without removing or contradicting elements → adjustment
Adds a new edge case to the risk list → adjustment

section.title: Strike counter does not penalize honest discovery
section.description: Reversals are NORMAL during D-phase — that's WHY D-phase is a firm checkpoint. The strike counter exists to catch ACCUMULATING incoherence (three reversals in one session signal the original Q↔R was wrong), not to discourage one honest course correction. Strike 1 is supposed to fire; strike 2 is a warning; strike 3 is the trigger.

section.title: The override option is intentional
section.description: Per Lock 31: "User can always insist on continuing past Strike 3." Some users have external time constraints or know context the agent does not (e.g., this is a throwaway exploration anyway). The override exists to honor that. But the bundle is then flagged as having been force-continued past abort threshold; ticket-verify will surface this for review.
